title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Recursive Language Models represent an inference-time compute paradigm that applies recursive principles to coding agents for improved performance and reliability. The presentation demonstrates how RLM concepts can be mapped onto coding agent architectures, including comparison with Claude Code sub-agents, and shows concrete applications in tools like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse for solving complex tasks with declarable outcomes.
writeup: Raymond Weitekamp of OpenProse presents Recursive Language Models as an inference-time compute paradigm and maps the recursive principles onto coding agent architectures. The talk compares this approach with Claude Code sub-agents and walks through concrete applications in Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse. The throughline is using recursion to handle complex tasks with declarable outcomes, aiming at improved performance and reliability for coding agents.
